title: Fluorescent 2 color Whole mount in situ hybridization for a mouse embryo
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"pgbdf","text":"New developed fluorescent 2 color Whole mount in situ hybridization produces a clear and high S/N ratio image to analyze mRNA localization inside 1 cell and in tissue levels.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":46,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"xwspq","text":"Whole mount in situ hybridization is a highly developed and common technique to reveal localizations of target mRNA in embryos 1). Although it can roughly tell us localization of mRNA in tissue levels, sometimes we need more precise information for its localization, especially inside one cell of tissues. Recently, many researchers are trying to stain mRNA with fluorescent reagents with whole embryos because fluorescence have higher resolutions than chromogenic substrate and it would tell us localizations of mRNA in 1 cell level. Some reports that used fluorescence methods gave us beautiful images and suggest important conclusions from localization patterns of mRNA inside cells. However, Whole mount fluorescence in situ hybridization is sometimes very difficult for researchers, because they have to modify the protocol for each species and each embryonic stages, if they want to take a beautiful image that have high S/N ratio. In this protocol, we describe how to perform fluorescent in situ hybridization in a mouse embryo. This protocol is originally based on Zebrafish fluorescence in situ hybridization method 2) , but a probe label is different from original one to achieve clearer image3). This protocol includes tips that reduce fluorescent background and you can adopt this method to Zebrafish and other animals also.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":12,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":721,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":995,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":1096,"length":7},{"style":"sup","offset":127,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":1125,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":1203,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fnvo4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ejntv","text":"Troubleshooting","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9mdt0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"agrg5","text":"At first, you must confirm expression patterns of mRNA by chromogenic method. If you can not obtain high S/N ratio with chromogenic ISH, it would be difficult to confirm signals by fluorescence ISH. \r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8lu6j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ef05j","text":"When background signal is high, confirm Tetrahydroborate step. Thick and large tissue might produce high background and you should cut tissues into small pieces.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: You can see green and red fluorescence from each probes.

Figure 1. Fluorescence 2 color whole mount in situ hybridization (a) Whole-mount FISH analysis of Cerl2 mRNA with Alexa-488 in the node region of a mouse embryo at 2pss. (b) Whole-mount FISH analysis as in (a) with a Nodal probe and Alexa555. Scale bars: 50 μm
Digest the plasmids with restriction enzyme which generate 5’ protruding terminus DNA 8 μg, 10x Buffer 10 μl, H 2 O 78 μl, Enzyme 2 μl \(2 hour) and 2μl \(2 hour).
Recover supernatant to new tube and add 300 μl Ethanol and 3M Sodium acetate.
Recover supernatant to new tube and add 100 μl chloroform. Vortex and centrifuge for 5 minutes at room temperature with 15,000 rpm.
Recover supernatant to new 1.5ml tube and then repeat step 4.
Add 2M Tris pH 7.5 6 μl and phenol-chloroform 100 μl, vortex and centrifuge for 5 minutes at room temperature with 15,000 rpm.
Add 4 µl of 5 mg/ml ProK and 10 µl of 10%SDS, then incubate for 30 minutes at 37℃.
Check the 1 µl reacted DNA in a gel and confirm a complete digestion.
Discard supernatant and dry the pellet. Dissolve 15 μl Tris-EDTA and store -20℃.
Discard supernatant and wash pellet with 80% Ethanol.
Mix with inverting a tube and centrifuge for 10 minutes at 4℃ with 15000 rpm.
For Dig labeled RNA probes Mix following solutions and incubate at 37℃ for 3 hours. %s %s
Check the synthesized quantity 1 hour after reaction by a gel running. If the quantity is small, add a little more enzyme.
After 3 hours, add following solutions directly into a reaction tube. 0.5M EDTA \(8.0) 1 μl 8M LiCl 1 μl EtOH 75 μl.
Mix well and immediately centrifuge at 15000 rpm for 10 minutes at 4℃.
80% EtOH wash.
Dry and dissolve in 20 μl TE.
Add following solutions 8M LiCl 1 μl EtOH 75 μl.
Mix well and immediately centrifuge at 15000 rpm for 10 minutes at 4℃.
80% EtOH wash.
Dry and dissolve in DPEC H 2 O 20 μl.
Store at -80 ℃.
For DNP labeled RNA probes Synthesis of Anti Sense probe ; Mix following solution and incubate at 37℃ 3 hours. %s %s
Add 6 μl DNase I \(10 u/μl) 6 μl and incubate for 15 minutes at 37℃.
Purify RNA probes by Roche G50 column.
Add 3 volumes EtOH and 3M Sodium Acetate and mix well.
Centrifuge at 4℃, 15000 rpm for 10 minutes.
Discard supernatant and wash pellet by 80%EtOH.
Repeat Step 26 and 27.
Dry a pellet and dissolve in 20 μl H 2 O.
DNP label \(Mirus Labeling kit); Mix following solutions and incubate at 37℃ for 2 hours. A B 1 H2O 33 μl 2 10×Buffer A 5 μl 3 AS 5 μl 4 Label IT Reagent 7 μl
Purify RNA probe by a column\(included in Miruskit).
Add 3 volumes EtOH and 3M Sodium Acetate and mix well.
Centrifuge at 4℃, 15000 rpm for 10 minutes.
Discard supernatant and wash pellet by 80%EtOH.
Dry a pellet and dissolve in 5 μl H 2 O.
Dissect the embryos out in DEPC-PBS and Fix in 4% PFA.
Rocking at 4℃ for 5 hours to overnight.
Wash for 5 minutes each with 25,50, 75% MeOH in PBT, then twice with 100%MeOH.
Rehydrate through this methanol/PBT series in reverse and wash twice with PBT.
Bleach with 6% hydrogen peroxidase in PBT for 1 hour.
Wash the embryos with PBT three times for 5 minutes.
Incubate overnight at 70℃ .
Change solution to Hybridiztion Mix \(probeA,DIG; 1μl + probeB, DNP;1μl / Prehybridization Mix solution 500μl).
PreHybridization for 30 minutes at 70℃ in Hybridization oven.
Change to PreHybridization Mix solution 2 times\(50% Formamide, 5xSSC pH5, 50 μg/ml Yeast RNA, 1%SDS, 50 μg/ml Heparin).
PBT wash 3 times.
Soak in freshly prepared 0.1% Sodium Borohydride / PBT solution for 30 minutes and repeat this step 4 times.
PBT wash 3 times.
Soak in 0.25% NH3 / 70% EtOH 20 minutes.
Wash 3 times with PBT.
Refix the embryos with 0.2% glutaraldehyde/ 4% PFA.
Tread with 10 μg/ml ProteinaseK in PBT for 15 minutes.
Wash the embryos twice with solution I \(50% Formamide, 5xSSC pH5, 1%SDS) for 30 minutes at 72℃.
Wash the embryos twice with solution III \(50% Formamide, 2xSSC pH5, 0.1%Tween20) for 30 minutes at 72℃.
Wash 3 times for 5 minutes with solution II \(0.5MNacl, 10mMTris-HCl pH7.5, 0.1%Tween20 )
Wash embryos with 100 μg/ml RNaseA in solution II for 30 minutes at 37℃.
Wash the embryos for 5 minutes with solution II, then with solution III.
Wash twice with solution III for 30 minutes at 67℃.
TBST wash 3 times.
Change to blocking solution \(0.1 M TrisHCl, pH 7.5, 0.15 M NaCl, 0.5% Blocking powder) and incubate at 4℃ with rocking for 1 hour.
Change to blocking solution 1 ml including anti-DIG-HRP\(Roche) 5 μl, then incubate with rocking at 4℃ overnight.
Wash 3 times for 5 minutes with TBST.
TBST wash 1 hour at 4℃ with rocking and repeat 5 times.
Change to Amplification Buffer \(Molecular Probe TSA kit.) then wait for a few minutes.
Changes to Amplification Buffer 1 ml including Tyramide-Alexa555 \(Molecular Probe TSA kit)1 μl, then incubate at 4℃ for 30 minutes in dark room.
Make 30% H2O2 1 μl in Amplification Buffer 200 μl \(solutionA).
Make Tyramide-Alexa555 1 μl, solutionA 1 μl and Amplification Buffer 100 μl \(solutionB).
Change to solutionB and incubate for 30 minutes at room temperature in a dark room.
TBST wash 3 times for 5 minutes.
Change to H2O including 0.1M Glysin, pH2.5 \(pH should be adjusted by 30%HCl) and incubate for 30 minutes at room temperature.
TBST wash 3 times for 5 minutes.
Change to blocking solution and incubate at 4℃ for 1 hour with rocking.
Change to blocking solution including Anti-DNP-HRP \(PerkinElmer) 1 μl and incubate at 4℃ for overnight with rocking.
Repeat of step 1-10 of Day 5.
Tyramide should be changed to Tyramide-Alexa488.
